title: Synaedrys fenestrata
taxonomy: Synaedrys fenestrata is classified as a species of plant in the family Ericaceae, commonly known as the heath family. The genus Synaedrys is an outdated and now largely superseded classification, with many species now placed in the genus Viburnum. Historically, Synaedrys fenestrata was a part of the European flora, with some classifications suggesting it was endemic to certain regions. However, as classification systems evolve, the plant's classification has undergone revisions. The plant is often now referred to as Viburnum lantana.

intro: Synaedrys fenestrata, also known as Ashitaba, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Apiaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, contributing to its potential in promoting digestive health, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Synaedrys fenestrata include chalcones, flavonoids, and coumarins, such as compound falcarinol, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Synaedrys fenestrata is commonly used to make tea infusions, extracts, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into supplements and functional foods to leverage its health benefits.
